def: v.t. To ask (of, -se), to inquire (of), to question, interrogate; to refer (to), to consult; to ask or inquire after (in this and the following senses pūćhnā governs the dative form of the accusative instead of the ablative); to care (about), feel concern (for); to mind, heed, regard, take notice (of); to invite; to help, assist
more: v. a. 1. To ask; question; put a question; interrogate; enquire; require an answer. 1. Pānī pī-ke zāt pūchhte ho? Prov. First drink his water and then ask his caste! 2. Mere ghoṛe kī kuchh na pūchho! Players' idiom. 3. Pūchhā kisī ne ye kisī kāmil faqīr se, 'Ye mehr o māh Haq ne banāë haiṅ kāhe ke?' Woh sun-ke bolā, Bābā, Khudā tujh-ko khair de, Ham to na chāṅd samjheṅ, na sūraj haiṅ jānte, Bābā, hameṅ to ye nazar ātī haiṅ roṭīyāṅ? ' On bread. Nazīr. A question one did put a perfect saint one day, 'The sun and moon how hath God made, say with what clay? He heard and said, 'Good Sir, God's blessing on your head! Nor sun nor moon I know, all I can see is bread? 4. Pūchhte pūchhte to Dillī pahuṅch jāte haiṅ . Prov. Ask oft your way and you may get to Delhi. (To ask is to learn). 2. To enquire after one's health or welfare. 3. To refer or make a reference to; consult. 4. To account; value; r…
v. a. To ask, to inquire, to question, to interrogate.

Cognate with Gujarati પૂછવું (pūchvũ), Marathi पुसणे (pusṇe), Kalasha phúčik, Romani pućhel, Persian پرسیدن (porsīdan), German forschen.

def: v.t. To honour, respect, venerate, to do homage (to), to reverence; to adore, worship; to idolatrize ( = pūjā karnā and pūjan karnā)
more: v. a. 1. To make an offering; to sacrifice; worship. 2. ( pūjnā ). To spend uselessly; throw away; sacrifice; lose. Chār rupaë pūj baiṭhe .
1. (for पूजन ) v. a. To worship, to adore, to venerate. 2. (for पूरण ) v. n. To be filled, completed, accomplished, fulfilled, perfected.
subs: v.t. To sacrifice, waste, spend (money, &c.) uselessly, to squander.

def: s.m. (f. -tā), The being entitled to honour or to worship, venerableness, honourableness.
v. a. To proclaim publickly, to call.
s.f. Calling out aloud; call, shout, bawl, halloo, noise, din; cry, outcry, summons; invitation; invocation; petition, suit, complaint, plaint; call (for), want or lack (of), need (of).
more: n. f. S. प्रकुश to call out. 1. Call; calling out ( bulāo ); cry for help; halloo. Kyā abr hai, chaman hai, hawā hai, bahār hai, Sāqī pahuṅch shitāb, ki terī pukār hai . Jurat. 2. Noise; din; shout; roar. Hāthī chhūṭā thān se, sāgar paṛī pukār, Sab darwāze band rahe, nikal gayā aswār . The soul. Dohā. But now held hard, broke from his stall—the world with clamour rent— The doors all closed, the rider 'scaped without impediment. 3. A summons; invitation. 4. Invocation; address; prayer. 5. Petition; suit; request ( darkhuāst ). 6. Complaint ( faryād ); plaint. Mālī āyā bāg meṅ, kalyan karī pukār, Phūlī phūlī ṭūṭ gaīṅ, kāl hamārī bār . Dohā. 'The gardener in the garden's come,' the young buds all exclaim, 'The flowers full-blown all plucked have been, to-morrow we the same!' 6. Want ( māṅg ); need; necessity. 7. Scarcity; drought; dearth.
s. f. Bawl, calling out aloud, cry, call.

def: s.m. Call, shout, &c. = pukār; noisiness, report, pretension, renown, fame, notoriety.
more: s. m. Call, cry, shout, noisiness, halloo, pretension, renown, report, fame.

def: s.f. Calling, crying out, &c. (see next).

def: adv. Aloud; openly, in public

ety: pukār˚ = S.स्फूत्कार or फूत्कार; Prk. पुक्कार˚
def: v.t. To call, call out to, to hail; to invoke; to invite; to summon; to publish, proclaim; to sue; to term, to denominate; — v.n. To call out, call alond; to cry (one's wares), to shout, bawl, to cry out, to exclaim; to lodge a complaint, to complain; to be called (as a roll-call)
more: v. n. 1. To call (بلانا 1, 2). 2. To cry ones wares. 3. To call aloud; bawl; cry out; exclaim; shout; yell; cry for help. 1. Māṅ māre, aur māṅ hī māṅ pukāre . Prov. The mother beats her child, the child still 'mother mother' cries. (The dog licks the hand that beats him). 2. Pagṛī bhī ādmī kī utāre hai ādmī, Chillā-ke ādmī ko pukāre hai ādmī . Man. Nazir. Who turbans make off with are also men, And men to whom for help cry other men. 3. Chhāḍo merā achrā maiṅ gārī dūṅgī, Sās, nanad ko pukār hī lūṅgī . Song. Thumrī. Let go my mantle's hem, I say, I will abuse you straight, I'll scream out to my mother-in-law and sister of my mate. 4. To invoke; call on God. 1. Nayā Musalmān 'Allāh hī Allāh' pukāre! Prov. A Musalmān just made! 'Allah, Allāh!' he cries all day. 2. Mohan rasyā āë bagyā meṅ, phūl rahī sab kalī kalī , Dohā. Koī kalī Hari nām japat hai, koī pukāre 'Ali, Alī .' The amorous Mohan…
v. a. To call aloud, to bawl, to cry out, exclaim, shout.
subs: v.t. To call, &c.; to publish, proclaim.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀸𑀭𑁂𑀤𑀺 (pukkāredi), from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀧𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀸𑀭𑁆- (*pukkār-, “shout”), ultimately onomatopoeic.

ety: perf. part. of puḵẖtan; rt. Zend pać; S. pać
def: part. adj. Dressed, cooked; baked (as brick, &c.); ripe, matured, mature; shrewd, knowing, expert. skilful, wise; firm, strong, solid well-built, of brick or stone, of masonry (syn. pakkā — puḵẖta-kārī, s.f. Ripeness, maturity; firm, or sound, or solid workmanship
more: adj. 1. Dressed (as victuals). 2. Baked (as bricks), of brick or stone. 3. Ripe. 4. Shrewd, knowing, expert, wise, firm, strong, solid. پخته کاري puḵẖta-kār-ī, s. f. Firm workmanship, maturity. پخته کرنا puḵẖta karnā, To make strong, shrewd or knowing, to ripen.
subs: v.t. To ripen; to make firm or strong; to make shrewd or knowing
adj. Of mature disposition, firm, experienced

def: s.f. The being cooked; ripeness, maturity; firmness, soundness, solidity, strength; experience (syn. pakkā-pan).
more: s. f. Ripeness, the being cooked, maturity, firmness; experience.

def: s. f. Firmness, constancy, stability.

s.f. Cakes, or pieces of bread, which are laid over dishes when bringing to table, and under which the meat is kept warm; cakes imbued with ghī or clarified butter (from being laid over certain greasy dishes, as do-piyāza, &c.: These cakes are very soft, rich, and savoury; and hence māmā puḵẖtariyāṅ khānā, 'to eat rich meal cakes prepared by his mother,' is a phrase employed to denote one who is accustomed to luxury, and incapable of labour or fatigue).
more: n. f. Wom. Rich meal cakes. Jis ne māmā pakhtaryāṅ khā/?/īṅ, us se kām nahīṅ hotā .

s. f. 1. Cakes (or bread) which are laid over dishes when bringing to table, and under which the meat is kept warm. 2. Cakes imbued with clarified butter from being laid over certain greasy dishes, such as dopiyāza, &c. (These cakes are very soft and savoury; whence) ماماپختريان کهانا māmā puḵẖtariyāṅ khānā, To eat cakes of this description prepared by his mother, is applied to a person accustomed to luxury and incapable of labour or fatigue.

ety: Old P. puhal; Pehl. puhar; Zend. peretu, fr. par = S. par
def: s.m. A bridge; a causeway, an embankment; — pul bāṅdhnā, v.n. To make a bridge (over, par), to bridge; to make an embankment or dike, to limit; to provide an abundant supply (of), to supply in abundance, to form a heap, to heap
more: n. m. A bridge. pul bāṅdhnā , v. a. 1. To make a bridge. 2. To raise a dam against inundation. 3. To supply abundantly; heap; cram. pul sarāt , n. m. Mah. A bridge along which on the day of judgment the righteous will pass to heaven, and the wicked fall into hell. pul-bandī , n. f. Keeping bridges or embankments in repair; a tax imposed for the purpose. pul ṭūṭ'nā , v. n. 1. To break (a bridge). 2. To burst forth in excess; to crowd.
s. m. A bridge, an embankment. پل باندهنا pul bāndhnā, v. a. ( lit. To make a bridge) 1. To limit. 2. To abound.
subs: s.f. Embankment; keeping bridges or embankments in repair; a tax levied for this purpose; — sodomy
(-kī), To commit sodomy; to ravish, violate; — pul ṭūṭnā, v.n. 'Bridge to break'; to burst or come forth en masse, to crowd
s.m. A bridge over which (in the belief of Mohammadans) the righteous will pass into Paradise, and from which the wicked fall into hell, on the day of judgment.
